items.title: ToddyCat: your hidden email assistant. Part 2
items.summary: An in-depth analysis of Umbrij, a new tool used by the ToddyCat APT group to compromise corporate email communications in Gmail. The attack targeted OAuth authorization tokens, allowing threat actors to gain access to Google services.

items.title: The AI Token Costs That Can Break Cybersecurity
items.summary:
As cybersecurity platforms embrace agentic AI, organizations must balance detection performance against the escalating costs of token consumption, deployment architecture, and AI credits.
The post The AI Token Costs That Can Break Cybersecurity appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Kali Linux 2026.2 released with 9 new tools, NetHunter updates
items.summary: Kali Linux 2026.2, the second release of the year, is now available for download, featuring 9 new tools and numerous Kali NetHunter improvements. [...]

items.title: Blackfield ransomware asks Nidec Corporation for $2 million ransom
items.summary: The Blackfield ransomware gang is asking for a $2 million ransom from Nidec Corporation, a large Japanese manufacturer of electronic components for automotive and computing applications. [...]

items.title: Nissan Employee Data Breached in Oracle PeopleSoft Hack
items.summary:
Only a handful of the 100 organizations targeted in the PeopleSoft campaign have been confirmed.
The post Nissan Employee Data Breached in Oracle PeopleSoft Hack appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Crypto exchange OKX wants AI agents to hire and pay each other
items.summary: OKX is bringing together payments, identity and reputation into a marketplace for AI agents.

items.title: CISA: Windows BlueHammer flaw now exploited by ransomware gangs
items.summary: CISA confirmed on Monday that ransomware gangs are now exploiting a Microsoft Defender privilege escalation vulnerability, dubbed BlueHammer, that has previously been abused in zero-day attacks. [...]

items.title: Critical SimpleHelp Vulnerability Exploited for Malware Delivery
items.summary:
The threat actor is focused on collecting credentials, SSH keys, cryptocurrency wallets, and development tooling.
The post Critical SimpleHelp Vulnerability Exploited for Malware Delivery appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: New BioShocking Attack Tricks AI Browsers Into Leaking User Credentials
items.summary:
Convince an AI browser that it is playing a game, and it can hand over your login details. That is the finding behind BioShocking, a technique from security firm LayerX that tricked six AI browsers and assistants into copying a user's credentials and sending them to an attacker.

The targets included OpenAI's ChatGPT Atlas, Perplexity's Comet, and Anthropic's Claude browser extension.

An

items.title: Progress Kemp LoadMaster Flaw Could Let Attackers Run Root Commands Pre-Auth
items.summary:
A critical vulnerability in Progress Kemp LoadMaster can let an unauthenticated attacker execute arbitrary commands as root on the appliance by sending a crafted request to its API.

The flaw, tracked as CVE-2026-8037, carries a CVSS score of 9.8 according to ZDI. A patch is available. If you run LoadMaster with the API enabled, update now.

Progress published its advisory on June

items.title: Apple Patches 30+ iOS, macOS, Safari Flaws, Including AI-Discovered WebKit Bugs
items.summary:
Apple on Monday released security updates for iOS, macOS, and the Safari web browser to address over three dozen flaws, including four vulnerabilities in WebKit that were discovered using artificial intelligence (AI) tools like Anthropic Claude and OpenAI Codex Security.

The WebKit vulnerabilities are listed below -

items.title: Quantifind Raises $200 Million for AI-Native Risk Intelligence
items.summary:
Quantifind will accelerate international expansion and extend its platform’s localized risk intelligence capabilities.
The post Quantifind Raises $200 Million for AI-Native Risk Intelligence appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Unlocking Britain’s next era of productivity: Building a nation of AI trailblazers
items.summary: Four illustrated characters representing different professional roles, including a scientist, technician, explorer, and observer.

items.title: New Controller Flaws Expose Highway Signs and Billboards to Remote Hacking
items.summary:
CISA has published an advisory to inform organizations about three vulnerabilities found by a researcher in Daktronics controllers.
The post New Controller Flaws Expose Highway Signs and Billboards to Remote Hacking appeared first on SecurityWeek.

items.title: Oracle E-Business Suite Flaw CVE-2026-46817 Actively Exploited in the Wild
items.summary:
A critical security flaw impacting Oracle E-Business Suite has come under active exploitation in the wild, according to Defused Cyber.

The vulnerability, tracked as CVE-2026-46817 (CVSS score: 9.8), refers to an improper privilege management and authentication flaw in Oracle Payments that could be abused to take over susceptible instances.

"Easily exploitable vulnerability allows

items.title: The AI jobs debate just got messier
items.summary: A new report finds "high-intensity AI adopters” saw headcount increase 10.2%. Among those companies, entry-level headcount rose by 12%, countering the rhetoric that AI kills junior jobs.

items.title: Vibe coding platform Base44 launches own model as AI startups seek defensibility
items.summary: Wix-owned vibe coding platform Base44 has started rolling out its own AI model — with hopes that it will eventually outperform frontier models.

items.title: HTML table extractor
items.summary:
Tool: HTML table extractor
        Yet another in my growing collection of paste-conversion tools. This one accepts pasted rich text from browsers (with embedded HTML tables) and converts every detected table into HTML, Markdown, CSV, TSV, or JSON.
Try it out by selecting everything on the Wikipedia List of cities and towns in the San Francisco Bay Area page and pasting it directly into the tool:

On a similar note, I recently rebuilt my Rich text to markdown tool to add support for tables and generally improve the UI.
Update: It turns out Wikipedia has an open CORS API for retrieving the full

items.title: 'Djinn' Stealer Targets Cloud, AI Credentials
items.summary: The infostealer was delivered via CVE-2026-48558, a critical authentication bypass vulnerability in SimpleHelp, targeting credentials linking development and admin environments to wider enterprise systems.

items.title: Vulnerabilities Expose Private Data in Indian Government Systems
items.summary: One critical vulnerability, among many discovered by a researcher, could have allowed anyone to walk in and take over a national government portal.

items.title: Nissan discloses employee data breach linked to Oracle zero-day attacks
items.summary: Nissan is warning that it suffered a data breach affecting current and former employees after threat actors exploited an Oracle PeopleSoft vulnerability in data theft attacks previously linked to the ShinyHunters extortion group. [...]

items.title: NAIC says public data stolen in ShinyHunters' PeopleSoft breach
items.summary: The National Association of Insurance Commissioners (NAIC) says the ShinyHunters extortion group stole only publicly available data, outdated logs, and configuration files after breaching its systems by exploiting a zero-day vulnerability in an Oracle PeopleSoft server. [...]
